findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:102-105` explicitly says infinite quotients give `0` by convention; the roadmap target is the class-group/genus-theory 2-rank in `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/Multiquadratic/README.md:127-132`, a finite class-group invariant.
findings.fix: Require `[Finite G]` on `TauCeti.twoRank` and put `TauCeti.ClassGroup.twoRank` under `[Finite (ClassGroup R)]`, or rename the unrestricted definition to make clear it is only `Module.finrank` and not the 2-rank invariant.
findings.issue: `twoRank` is defined for every `CommGroup G`, so infinite elementary-2 quotients get the misleading value `0` from `Module.finrank`; the class-group wrapper at `TauCeti/NumberTheory/ClassGroup/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:76` inherits the same too-general API.

summary: The quotient construction is at a natural general level, but the exported `twoRank` invariant is over-generalized beyond the finite setting where it matches the class-group 2-rank used by the roadmap.
